ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON SEP-11-2018 IN 503, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIES ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(D) AND (E). THE TRIAL DATE IS DECEMBER 31 AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(F) IS JANUARY 9, 2019. LOCAL RULE 20 SHALL BE FOLLOWED BY THE PARTIES AS WELL AS ALL SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULES AND CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS DECEMBER 27, 2018. FACT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS DECEMBER 14, 2018. ON AGREEMENT OF COUNSEL, EXPERT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS OPEN UNTIL A JURY IS SEATED. FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON NOVEMBER 14, 2018 AT 1:30P.M. PARTIES MAY APPEAR AT THE CONFERENCE BY TELEPHONE. IF THE PARTIES AGREE THAT NO CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS NECESSARY, THEY SHALL NOTIFY THE COURT AT LEAST TWO DAY PRIOR TO TAKE THE CMC OFF CALENDAR. MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WITH ONLY THE ATTORNEYS PRESENT AND FINAL DECISION MAKERS AVAILABLE BY PHONE IS SET FOR THURSDAY, DECEMBER 27, 2018 AT 9:30 A.M. IN 603. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CYNTHIA MING-MEI LEE, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: ANGELA SINCLAIR, CSR 13902 (AIKEN WELCH, TEL: 877.451.1580/EMAIL: ASINCLAIR@AIKENWELCH.COM)

calendar.matters:
Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Marine Dockside Industrial, Inc.
Off Calendar Mar-21-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Marine Dockside & Industrial, Inc.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703. Email: mlyon@sftc.org.

Geoff T. Sloniker, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Kurt Ellis and Shaoqin Ellis, who were not present at this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendant Marine Dockside & Industrial, Inc.
Plaintiffs' complaint for personal injury/loss of consortium was filed on December 22, 2017.
Amendment to complaint substituting Marine Dockside & Industrial, Inc. for DOE 303 was filed March 16, 2018.
Summons, Complaint, Amendment to Complaint and Statement of Damages were personally served on defendant Marine Dockside & Industrial, Inc.'s Agent on April 11, 2018.
Default as to defendant Marine Dockside & Industrial, Inc. was entered on June 1, 2018.
Statement of Damages as to defendant Marine Dockside & Industrial, Inc. was filed on June 1, 2018.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on February 15, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Kurt Ellis and Shaoqin Ellis, and against the Defaulted Defendant Marine Dockside & Industrial, Inc., as follows:

Economic Damages as to Plaintiff Kurt Ellis - $1,510,205.50.
Non-Economic Damages as to plaintiff Kurt Ellis - $10,000,000.00.
Loss of Consortium as to plaintiff Shaoquin Ellis - $10,000,000.00.
Total - $21,510,205.50.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Marine Dockside Industrial, Inc.
Off Calendar Feb-21-2019 Continued To Mar-21-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514

ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-12-2018 Set For Trial On Dec-31-18 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Nov-14-2018 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Sep-11-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: (Minutes Part 1 of 2)
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Appearances: Kimberly Chu of Brayton Purcell LLP (415.898.1555) for plaintiffs and moving parties; Constance Fraenkel of Spanos Przetak (no card provided) designated defense counsel; Paul Smith of Hugo Parker (415.808.0300) for defendants Foster Wheeler and Polar Tankers; Samantha Adhikari of Walsworth WFBM, LLP (415.544.7143) for defendants Dee and Sun Ship; Eric D. Sentlinger of Pond North LLP (415.217.1240) for defendant Genuine Parts Company; Kyle Clawson of Dentons (415.882.5000) for defendants Pep Boys and Metalclad Insulation; Thomas A. Steig of Prindle, Goetz, Barnes & Reinholtz LLP (415.788.8354) for defendants Syd Carpenter and Marine Construction, Inc.; Steven Cross of Becherer Kannett & Schweitzer (510.658.3600 x243) appearing via Court Call for defendant M. Slayen.

Argued and the Court modifies its ruling as follows: Granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(d) and (e). The trial date is December 31 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is January 9, 2019. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is December 27, 2018. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is December 14, 2018. On agreement of counsel, expert discovery cut-off date is open until a jury is seated. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than October 9, 2018. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. (Minutes Continued in Part 2 of 2)

Motion For Preference Setting Case For Trial And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only.)
Off Calendar Sep-11-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: (Continued Minutes - Part 2 of 2) No later than September 25, 2018 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than September 25, 2018 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on November 14, 2018 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than November 16, 2018. Responses to the demand are due no later than November 30, 2018. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by December 21, 2018 at 12:00p.m. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Thursday, December 27, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. in 603. Order signed. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Angela Sinclair, CSR 13902 (Aiken Welch, Tel: 877.451.1580/Email: asinclair@aikenwelch.com) (503/CML)
